blocks.text: DTC P0712: Transmission Fluid temperature sensor short circuit

The transmission fluid temperature sensor uses a thermistor type whose resistance changes with the temperature of the transmission fluid. The PGM-FI ECU supplies a 5 V voltage to the temperature sensor through a pull-up register. When the transmission fluid temperature is low, the temperature sensor resistance increases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a higher voltage; when the transmission fluid temperature is high, the temperature sensor resistance decreases and the PGM-FI ECU detects a lower voltage. If the detected voltage remains below the specified value (transmission fluid temperature is above the specified value) for more than the specified time, the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: 24-hour (e.g. observation, operation (of a machine), care (of patient))
Detection order (of the DTC in question)
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: off, D indicator light: flashing
Normal judgment, failure judgment, not completed
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Transmission Fluid temperature sensor output voltage [HMMF temperature sensor (V)] remains below 0.07V for more than 10 seconds.

items.text: Ground fault in code (TATF line) between PGM-FI ECU and transmission fluid temperature sensor
Turn the select lever to the P position and wait for at least 10 seconds.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the D indicator light will flash without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the DTC and freeze data will be stored.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
